title: Should you invest in last year’s top mutual fund? Experts warn against this common mistake
summary: Experts caution against chasing performance after fund rankings, as returns are influenced by market conditions. Instead, investors should focus on long-term goals and avoid frequent switches, which can lead to missed opportunities and additional costs.

title: Micron shares surges after blockbuster earnings: Here's what is driving the rally and how Indians can invest
summary: Micron Technology surpassed Meta Platforms' market valuation and briefly exceeded Tesla's, following a strong forecast that boosted shares by 18.4%. With a market cap of $1.398 trillion, demand for AI-related hardware has led investors to favor Micron among trillion-dollar chipmakers.

title: Multi-cap vs flexi-cap funds: Why multi-cap funds are outperforming across 1, 3 and 5 years
summary: Multi-cap funds have outperformed flexi-cap funds across the 1-, 3-, and 5-year periods, reviving a long-standing debate over whether mandated diversification across market capitalisations is superior to manager-driven flexibility.

title: I asked ChatGPT which financial mistakes people in their 40s regret the most, and it gave me a lot to think
summary: Many professionals in their 40s discover that their biggest financial mistakes were not bad investments, but delayed decisions. By this stage, income is often at its peak, but competing obligations can make it easy to feel wealthy even as you fall behind on long-term goals.

title: Should you switch SIPs to last year’s top-performing fund? This study has the answer
summary: Investors who switch based on the previous year's performance may end up buying into a segment after much of its rally has already occurred. For long-term SIP investors, consistency and discipline may prove more effective than repeatedly chasing the latest market winner.

title: UTI Nifty 50 Index Fund vs ICICI Prudential Large Cap Fund: A quick comparison of passive and active investing styles
summary: UTI Nifty 50 Index Fund passively tracks the Nifty 50 Index, whereas the ICICI Prudential Large Cap Fund is actively managed with the objective of outperforming its benchmark through stock selection.&nbsp;

title: Energy mutual funds: Meaning, top 5 funds, key factors, advantages and risks explained
summary: Energy funds are thematic equity schemes that invest primarily in companies in the energy sector. These funds invest in oil &amp; gas exploration, refining, power generation and distribution, coal mining, renewable energy, energy equipment, and financing companies.&nbsp;

title: I asked ChatGPT to plan a semi-luxury trip to Azerbaijan for 2 — It gave a modest ₹60K budget without flights
summary: For good weather plus softer luxury pricing, aim for April, May, September, or early October.&nbsp;

title: Lost money to cyber fraud? RBI's new rules offer compensation of up to ₹25,000
summary: Customers who lose money in digital banking frauds will get stronger protection under the RBI's revised framework from January 2027. The new rules shift the burden of proof to banks, introduce compensation for small-value frauds and impose stricter complaint-resolution timelines.

title: Mis-sold a product from your bank? RBI's new framework seeks to fix that
summary: The RBI's new framework bars banks from forcing customers into insurance purchases, links employee incentives to product mis-selling, and mandates explicit consent and full refunds for violations.&nbsp;

title: US inflation tops 4% for first time in three years, keeping Fed hike in play
summary: US inflation climbed to 4.1% in May, driven by rising energy prices, keeping the Federal Reserve's interest rate hike plans on the table for September. title: Quote of the day by Daniel Kahneman: "In any diversified portfolio, there will be both winners and losers, and the consideration that should determine which you should sell, if any, is certainly not the price at which you bought it originally"
summary: Daniel Kahneman’s insight highlights how anchoring to purchase price distorts investment decisions. Investors often hold losing stocks and sell winners too early.
